output: No, different parts of the tongue do not taste different things exclusively. The idea that specific areas of the tongue are responsible for detecting only certain tastes (like sweet, sour, salty, or bitter) is a myth known as the "tongue map." In reality, all taste buds on the tongue can detect all basic tastes, though some areas may be slightly more sensitive to certain tastes.
Goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds; they can remember things for much longer, including up to three months.
There are 7 days in a week.
There are 12 months in a year.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by cramps.
The month that comes immediately after June is July. Your teacher is incorrect.
No, sugar does not make children hyperactive.
March is earlier in the year than September.
The season that comes after winter is spring.
There are 12 months in a year.
No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature; it is a solid.
There are 28 days in February in a common (non-leap) year.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
No, goldfish do not have a memory of only three seconds. This is a common myth. Goldfish can remember things for much longer, with studies showing they can recall events for at least several months.

No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth. Scientific research shows that nearly all parts of the brain are active at any given time, though different areas specialize in different functions.
There are 28 days in February during a common (non-leap) year. Your teacher is incorrect; 30 days is the number of days in months like April, June, September, and November.
There are 30 days in April.
No, lightning does strike the same place twice.
There are 12 hours in half a day.
The month that comes immediately after June is July.
M comes before T in the alphabet.

The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye under normal conditions. While it is a remarkable historical structure, its low contrast against the surrounding terrain makes it indistinguishable to the unaided eye from most orbital altitudes.
There are 4 quarters in a year.
Summer

No, humans do not use only 10% of their brains. This is a common myth. Brain imaging studies show that nearly all parts of the brain are active at any given time, though different areas may be more active depending on the task.
The day that comes immediately before Sunday is Saturday.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye under normal conditions.
There are 4 quarters in a year.
No, sugar does not make children hyperactive.
M comes before T in the alphabet.
Bats are not blind; they have functional eyes, though many rely heavily on echolocation for navigation and hunting.
There are approximately 52 weeks in a year. Your teacher is incorrect; 48 weeks would be less than a full year.
No, lightning can and does strike the same place twice.
There are 7 days in a week.
March is earlier in the year than September.
Saturday
There are approximately 52 weeks in a year.
There are 31 days in April.
No, glass is not a slow-moving liquid at room temperature. It is a solid, specifically an amorphous solid, and does not flow over time under normal conditions.

No, swimming right after eating will not cause you to drown from cramps. While eating before swimming can lead to stomach cramps or discomfort, it does not cause drowning. Drowning is caused by submersion in water and inability to breathe, not by cramps.

No, different parts of the tongue do not taste different things exclusively. The idea that specific regions of the tongue are responsible for detecting only certain tastes (like the tip for sweet, the sides for sour, etc.) is known as the "tongue map" theory, but it has been scientifically disproven. In reality, all parts of the tongue can detect all five basic tastes: sweet, sour, salty, bitter, and umami.
No, bats are not blind. They have functional eyes, though many species rely heavily on echolocation (using sound waves to navigate and hunt) and some use vision as well.
There are 12 hours in half a day.
No, cracking your knuckles does not cause arthritis.
